footer.productMethodology: Every conclusion carries an evidence grade — the "Measured" label is reserved for L3/L4 evidence.
footer.evidenceLevels: Evidence grades L0–L4: from unsupported to hard-measured, conclusions are labeled by evidence strength and never overstated.
footer.sameProtocol: Same-protocol retest: before/after comparisons reuse the same prompt set, market/language, model family and sampling rule.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Shared services and API keys (BYOK). These credentials are used by diagnostic runs across all projects.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Manage each client site you own and revisit past diagnostic reports.
projects.empty: No projects yet — start a new analysis.

projectDetail.noRuns: This project has no runs yet.
projectDetail.gscNotConnected: Not connected — without GSC there's no L4 measured clicks/impressions/position.
projectDetail.gscNotConfiguredHint: The platform connection service is not ready. Please contact the platform administrator.
projectDetail.gscPlatformNotReadyTitle: GSC platform connection is not ready
projectDetail.siteSelectionLoading: Loading properties this Google account can access…
projectDetail.siteSelectionEmpty: This Google account has no selectable GSC properties. Grant it access to the target property in Search Console, then reconnect.
projectDetail.siteSelectionError: We could not load authorized properties. Reconnect GSC and try again.

screen1.intro: Enter a URL and Veris runs an SEO + GEO diagnosis on real data (your GSC, measured answers from each AI engine, page crawls) — every conclusion comes with verifiable evidence.
screen1.competitorsPlaceholder: e.g. Competitor A, Competitor B, Competitor C (comma-separated)
screen1.enginesLabel: Probe engines (proxy-metric source for GEO visibility · developer-API sampling)
screen1.gscDesc: Once connected, see your real rankings, impressions and clicks — the most accurate ground truth for diagnosis
screen1.briefTitle: Confirm the diagnostic boundary before collection
screen1.briefGscOff: Page crawl; GSC is not collected for this run
screen1.briefFlowValue: Collect evidence → create findings → human review → output prompts
screen1.briefNote: This setup is saved with the project so retests can use the same protocol and stay comparable.
screen1.step1Sub: Enter a domain — we'll smart-fill the rest, editable anytime.
screen1.step2Sub: The more real sources you connect, the wider the coverage; you can also skip.
screen1.step3Sub: Confirm the diagnostic boundary, then start collecting.
screen1.competitorsOptionalHint: Leave blank — AI auto-discovers candidates you confirm later on the competitors page.
screen1.gscImpact: Not connected: no real rankings / clicks / impressions (L4 measured ground truth)
screen1.gscRecommendationTitle: Recommended: connect GSC for measured search data
screen1.gscRecommendationDetail: It adds clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position. Read-only access stays bound to this project so the diagnosis has a fuller evidence base.
screen1.gscPropertyRequired: Your Google account is authorized, but you still need to choose an authorized GSC property from the project detail page. This diagnosis will not collect GSC data yet.
screen1.gscNotConfiguredHint: The platform connection service is not ready. Please try again later or contact the platform administrator.
screen1.aiProbeDesc: Measured sample answers from each AI's developer API (ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini / DeepSeek) — a proxy metric, not what a consumer sees in the actual product UI
screen1.aiProbeImpact: Not configured: AI visibility, answer map and share-of-voice will be empty
screen1.skipHint: Any source can be skipped for now; connect GSC later from the project detail page, or configure other BYOK sources in Settings before re-running.
screen1.estimateDisclaimer: An estimate — actual usage governs; the real protocol is visible after collection.
screen1.submitError: Couldn't start the analysis. Please try again.
screen1.errorInvalidDomain: Invalid site URL. Please check it and try again.
screen1.errorDispatchFailed: Failed to dispatch the collection job: the background job service (Inngest) is unavailable. Start it and try again.
screen1.note: Diagnosis is asynchronous: only evidence collected from real sources reaches the next screen; unconfigured sources stay pending.

screen2.uncollectedHint: The crawl source is ready; this run hasn't collected evidence for this dimension yet
screen2.perEngineMeta: AI engines cite differently (~11–13% overlap) — reported per engine, not merged.
screen2.sentimentMeta: Sentiment of brand mentions (measurement-layer classifier; n=5 directional).
screen2.overviewBody: This run has {evidence} evidence artifacts and {findings} findings. Review the evidence first, then confirm recommendations.
screen2.nextReview: Review issues and evidence first, then confirm recommendations

run.collectingDetail: Page crawl, structured data and render comparison evidence will stream in; this page refreshes when complete.
run.readyDetail: You can review findings and confirm recommendations now; every measured value opens to evidence.

reviewGate.pendingDetail: Collection and diagnosis are complete (100%). Output starts only after all {total} recommendations are accepted, edited, or rejected.
reviewGate.emptyTitle: No recommendations are ready to confirm
reviewGate.emptyDetail: Collection and diagnosis are complete (100%), but this run has no recommendations to send to output. Review the diagnosis to decide whether more recommendations are needed.

screen2.sovMeta: Share of mentions for you and competitors across all probe answer samples
screen2.emptyFindings: Page evidence collected this run; automatic finding generation is not connected yet
screen2.mapMeta: Two zones, two different measurements: unbranded prompts test unprompted recall, branded prompts test cognitive quality — select any cell to inspect the raw answer
screen2.mapProxyNote: Methodology note: data on this page comes from developer-API sampling of ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini / DeepSeek — a proxy metric reflecting what the model can determine, not what a consumer actually sees in the corresponding product's web/app UI (no personalization, plugins, or live browsing). A measured-exposure protocol for Google's AI Overviews will ship once DataForSEO is integrated.
screen2.mapEvidenceTitle: Does AI bring you up on its own, without your brand name in the prompt
screen2.mapEvidenceDetail: Color only records whether any real answer for that (brand-name-free) prompt mentions your brand on its own. It is not a rank or score. Small samples swing a lot run to run — see the 95% lower bound below. Select a cell to inspect the question and source answers.
screen2.mapOutcomeHeadlineMissing: AI did not bring up your brand in any unbranded prompt
screen2.mapOutcomeHeadlinePresent: AI brought up your brand in some unbranded prompts
screen2.mapOutcomeHeadlineUnmeasured: There are no unbranded prompts to assess unprompted recall
screen2.mapOutcomeSummaryMissing: When people ask only about a category or solution, AI is not currently including you in its answer.
screen2.mapOutcomeSummaryPresent: When people ask only about a category or solution, you appear in some AI answers, but coverage remains limited.
screen2.mapOutcomeSummaryUnmeasured: Check the probe setup and ensure it includes category prompts without your brand name.
screen2.mapOutcomeEvidence: Based on {count} sampled prompts in this run
screen2.mapEvidenceAnswers: {count} AI answers sampled in this run
screen2.mapEvidencePrompts: Among unbranded prompts, brand appears in {present}/{total}
screen2.mapEvidenceGridLabel: AI answer evidence index by prompt
screen2.mapEvidenceGridHint: Each cell is a real prompt without your brand name
screen2.mapEvidenceNoAnswers: This prompt has no inspectable AI answer in this run, so it is not counted as absent.
screen2.mapEvidenceAnswerUnavailable: This sample has no displayable raw answer text.
screen2.legendSelect: Select a cell to inspect the prompt and source answers
screen2.mapWilsonNote: 95% lower confidence bound {pct}% — small n means single-run numbers swing; call it a real "change" only when two rounds' intervals don't overlap
screen2.mapOpportunityBody: Zero unprompted hits early on is normal for a smaller brand. Check the competitor SoV comparison and per-engine breakdown below to find content and structured-fact gaps to close.
screen2.mapBrandedTitle: When AI talks about your brand, is it grounded
screen2.mapBrandedDetail: This measures the model's cognitive quality about a known brand name: does it cite grounding, honestly admit it doesn't know, or assert without any backing. Non-web-search engines (e.g. DeepSeek) structurally cannot cite sources, so they're excluded from the grounded/unverified split — only flagged as speculative, unknown, or undetermined.
screen2.mapBrandedAnswers: {count} branded-prompt answers sampled in this run
screen2.mapBrandedEmpty: No branded-prompt probe answers in this run.
screen2.mapBrandedNoWebSearch: This engine cannot cite sources — excluded from the grounded/unverified judgment
screen2.engineMemoryHint: Reflects training-data memory — unlikely to shift over a short retest cycle
screen2.geoHeadline: Unbranded recall {present}/{total} · {speculative} branded answers look speculative
screen2.sovPerEngineMeta: Share of mentions computed separately per AI engine (engines aren't interchangeable — listed separately)
screen2.citedDomainsMeta: Distribution of domains actually cited in AI answers (not just retrieved), by count
screen2.findingsMeta: Expand any item to see the raw evidence
screen2.note: Only real data collected or generated for the current run is shown; GSC / AI probes / diagnostic generation remain empty until connected.

configHint.search_provider: Set GOOGLE_CSE_API_KEY and GOOGLE_CSE_CX in .env, then re-run the diagnosis
configHint.ai_probe: Set any AI key in .env (OPENAI_API_KEY / PERPLEXITY_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY / DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) and select the engine when creating an analysis
configHint.gsc: Connect a Google account and choose an authorized GSC property from the project detail page, then re-run the diagnosis
configHint.render_fallback: This run captured initial HTML and performance evidence in fallback mode; no rendered DOM was captured, so JavaScript content delta cannot be calculated. Browser rendering is optional and does not require the audited site to use Cloudflare.
configHint.uncollected: The crawl source is ready; re-run the diagnosis to collect it

screen2.probePending: No measured AI probe data yet · set any AI key in .env (OPENAI_API_KEY / PERPLEXITY_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY / DEEPSEEK_API_KEY), select the engine when creating an analysis, then re-run
screen2.probePendingRerun: AI keys configured ({providers}); this run has no probe data yet — re-run the diagnosis to see real data here
screen2.aioExposureTitle: Does the AI Overview cite your domain
screen2.aioExposureSectionMeta: Real SERP sampling · contrast with the API proxy-metric sections above
screen2.aioExposureDetail: Data is sampled from real Google search result pages: each query is actually fetched from the SERP to determine whether an AI Overview appears and whether its citations hit your own domain — not a model's self-report. This is the only exposure metric in the product allowed to be labeled "measured."
screen2.aioExposureEmptyConfigImpact: DataForSEO API key is not configured, so real SERPs cannot be collected — this card will stay empty
screen2.aioExposureEmptyUncollected: DataForSEO is configured, but this run hasn't collected AI Overviews exposure data yet. Re-run the diagnosis to see real sampled results here.
screen2.aioExposureSampleNote: Single-shot sample (n=1); actual exposure fluctuates day to day — no confidence interval is shown since the sample size doesn't support one; a retest needs several rounds to see a trend
screen2.aioExposureNoDomains: The AI Overview did not cite any domain this run
screen2.aioExposureNoQueries: No query records to show this run
screen2.aioExposureOwnedCited: The AI Overview cited your own domain
screen2.aioExposureNotOwnedCited: The AI Overview did not cite your own domain
screen2.aioExposureNoCitedUrls: No citation URLs were collected for this query

screen3.title: Recommendations
screen3.meta: Ranked by "impact × confidence ÷ effort" · each must be confirmed before output
screen3.noRationale: No additional rationale was recorded. Use the evidence and validation plan to decide.
screen3.empty: No recommendations yet this run; automatic finding-to-recommendation generation is not connected yet
screen3.note: Human in the loop: the tool proposes, you decide. Accepted / edited recommendations enter the final execution report; rejected decisions remain traceable but never enter the execution plan. Off-site recommendations (e.g. forums) only produce talking-point drafts, never auto-executed.

screen4.title: Output
screen4.note: A delivery draft is not a publication: preview or edit its Markdown here, then hand it to an executor. Mark it executed only after the CMS or code change is live so the same-protocol retest can be scheduled.

output.retestPending: Mark every action executed to start the same-protocol retest (4–6 weeks).
output.retestReady: Every action is executed — you can schedule the same-protocol retest.
output.retestPlanNoDue: No retest scheduled yet (no recommendation has been marked executed)
output.retestPlanPolicyNote: Rule: marking any recommendation executed pushes the retest due date to 28 days after that action; undoing execution does not roll it back.
output.retestPlanManualNote: Manually adjusting the due date isn't supported yet: the project update API doesn't expose this field, and extending it is outside this change's scope.
output.factsGateEmpty: No verified brand facts yet — execution prompts have no real facts to inject.

provenanceHint.i: Inferred: derived from evidence patterns, not directly verified
provenanceHint.g: Gap: an area flagged for improvement

findings.legend: Evidence grade legend: Measured = backed by L3/L4 hard evidence; Inferred = derived from evidence patterns, not directly verified; Gap = flagged for improvement; Good = already verified as meeting the bar

facts.title: Brand facts
facts.meta: Only "verified" brand facts are injected into execution prompts (§6.2 human-in-the-loop gate)
facts.empty: No brand facts yet. Add one, then verify it manually before it can be injected into prompts.
facts.gateNotice: "Verify" = a human confirms this fact is true and verifiable, authorizing its injection into execution prompts; unverified brand claims are never injected.
facts.sourceNotePlaceholder: e.g. Official About page, checked on 2026-07-19

competitors.title: Competitors
competitors.subtitle: SERP-overlap candidates. Confirm the ones that are real organic competitors — only confirmed competitors enter gap analysis and comparison (human-in-the-loop).
competitors.noData: No competitor candidates yet. Configure DataForSEO and re-run to auto-identify competitors from SERP overlap.
competitors.reevalNotice: Confirming triggers incremental re-evaluation (gap keywords, SoV, backlinks) merged into the current run.
competitors.matrixEmpty: Confirm at least one competitor to see the comparison.
competitors.estimateNote: Overlap & keyword figures are third-party estimates (L3).

keywords.title: Keyword status
keywords.subtitle: GSC-measured keyword metrics (L4) and DataForSEO gap analysis.
keywords.empty: No keyword data for this run yet.
keywords.emptyGscImpact: No keyword data for this run yet. Connect Google Search Console to get measured search performance metrics.
keywords.emptyGscCta: Connect GSC from the project detail page
keywords.emptyDataforseoImpact: Configure a DataForSEO API key to get keyword gap analysis (third-party estimate, L3).

terms.canonical: A canonical tag marks which URL is the "official" one among a batch of duplicate-looking pages. If it mistakenly points to another site, your page's ranking credit can end up going to that other site instead of getting indexed itself.
terms.noindex: noindex is a "don't index me" instruction on a page. If a page that should be searchable gets mistakenly tagged this way, it will never show up in Google search results — invisible to visitors.
terms.http4xx: 4xx is a server response code meaning the request failed (e.g. 404 = page not found). These links are likely broken — visitors hit an error page, and search engines read it as a bad experience.
terms.orphanPages: An orphan page has no other page linking to it. Search engines mostly discover and evaluate content through links between pages, so an unlinked page is hard to crawl, index, or rank.
terms.httpStatus: The HTTP status code is the server's response to this request: 200 means OK, 301/302 means it redirected elsewhere, 404 means the page doesn't exist, 5xx means the server errored. Anything other than 200 usually needs a look.
terms.urlPattern: Pages with a similar URL structure (e.g. all product detail pages) are automatically grouped into one template, so you can spot-check a single "representative page" instead of checking every page in that group.
terms.renderDelta: Unit: characters. Value = main-content character count after browser JS rendering minus the character count in the initial HTML response. The larger the positive number, the more this page's core content depends on JS to display — and search crawlers may not wait for JS to finish, so they may miss it.
terms.wilsonLowerBound: A number obtained by conservatively narrowing a small-sample measurement, so a lucky one-off result doesn't get mistaken for the site's real rate. The smaller the sample, the further this number sits from the raw hit rate.
terms.reportLevel: R0-R5 marks how far this report can responsibly answer right now — not a score. It depends on which data sources were actually collected this run; a higher level means a more complete evidence chain.
terms.dataforseo: DataForSEO is a third-party SEO data provider this tool uses for keyword competition, competitor analysis, backlinks, and real Google AI Overviews search sampling. It needs an API key configured in Settings before any of that data can be collected — until then, the related sections stay empty, which isn't a bug.
terms.labData: This data comes from PageSpeed Insights' simulated lab environment (a fixed device/network condition), not real visitors' actual experience. It's still "measured" evidence (really measured, not guessed) — just measured in a lab setting, not your visitors' real-world conditions.
terms.claimGrounded: This AI answer came with a checkable citation link — the claim has a traceable source.
terms.claimSpeculative: The AI answer hedges (e.g. "might", "reportedly") without giving a verifiable source.
terms.claimUnverified: The AI stated this confidently, but this engine can search the web and still gave no source link — more concerning than "speculative"; don't take it at face value.
terms.claimUndetermined: This AI engine has no web-search capability at all (it only answers from training memory), so it can't be held to the same "show your source" bar as a search-backed engine — it's neither grounded nor unverified, just its own category.

site.title: Site structure
site.truncatedNotice: Reached the {maxPages}-page cap; {count} URLs left unchecked
site.nextRunNotice: Changes take effect on the next run
site.noData: Site crawl was not enabled for this run or collection has not finished

report.title: Comprehensive Diagnostic Report
report.rulesUpgradedBanner: The rule library upgraded from {from} to {to}. This report was generated under the older ruleset; affected rules are not directly comparable — re-run under the current rules.
report.generatedBy: Generated by Veris · evidence-based SEO + GEO diagnosis

summary.breakdownExplainIntro: The health score (inferred) is a weighted average of five pillar scores (P1 Technical health, P2 Structured data, P3 Keywords & content, P4 SERP competition, P5 Brand authority & GEO), each scored 0–100. A pillar with no collected evidence is excluded from the average entirely — it is not scored as 0, nor does it drag the total down. The raw breakdown below shows exactly how many points each pillar lost and why.
summary.breakdownExplainRelation: The health score and the "severity" label on each finding below (high/mid/low) are two different rulers: severity measures how urgent one specific issue is, while the health score is a site-wide weighted overview — neither replaces the other. To decide what to fix first, look at findings marked "high" severity. Use the health score only to gauge overall direction; it is inferred, not measured, and should not be used alone to make decisions.
summary.noFindings: No issues found this run — this may mean the site performs well within the checked scope, or it may indicate incomplete evidence coverage. Check 'Diagnostic Scope & Coverage' to confirm data source status.

l0.name: Unsupported
l0.desc: No evidence; not allowed as a conclusion

constraint.systemic_basics: Your site currently has severe problems where search engines "can't see it" or "can't see it fully" — pages that won't load, aren't indexed, or render blank. Fix these first, or later optimization work is mostly wasted.
constraint.visibility_data_missing: There isn't enough data yet to see how your site actually performs on keywords — connect Google Search Console or configure DataForSEO first so the diagnosis can be accurate.
constraint.authority_content: The site's foundation is fine, but it covers too few keywords and brand recognition in the industry is still weak — focus next on adding content and building reputation.
constraint.fine_tuning: The site is in good overall shape with no systemic blockers — this is now a fine-tuning stage: work through the items below in order of "high impact, low cost."

contract.scopeMeta: The report first states what it can and cannot answer, preventing blanks from being mistaken for a clean bill of health.

levelDesc.R0: Collection status: shows data source gaps and collection failures only
levelDesc.R1: Technical discoverability: can assess technical, structural and rendering risks
levelDesc.R2: SEO performance: can analyze query/page performance, CTR and ranking opportunities
levelDesc.R3: SEO market opportunity: can analyze demand, competitor samples and keyword gaps
levelDesc.R4: GEO sample visibility: can assess brand sample visibility in AI search
levelDesc.R5: Business priority: can rank by lead/deal impact

contract.gapHint: The following data sources are unconfigured or failed; conclusions for these dimensions are unavailable:

geo.title: GEO Visibility Supplement
geo.meta: Brand visibility and cognitive quality in AI search — n=5 directional sampling
geo.wilsonNote: Small samples swing a lot round to round; call it a real change only when the two rounds' intervals don't overlap — otherwise it's noise.
geo.brandedHeadline: Of {brandedTotal} branded prompts, {grounded} grounded · {speculative} look speculative
geo.citationRate: Citation rate among search-backed engine answers: {pct}%
geo.empty: No AI probe data for this run.
geo.probeProxyNote: Scope note: the four AI probes above (ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini / DeepSeek) are sampled via developer APIs — a proxy metric for what the model can judge, not measured real-world exposure.
geo.aioMeta: Data is sampled from real Google search result pages, distinct from the four AI probes' proxy-metric scope above — the only exposure metric in this product allowed to be labeled "measured."
geo.aioEmptyNotConfigured: DataForSEO data source is not configured; real SERP sampling is unavailable, so this section stays empty
geo.aioEmptyNotCollected: DataForSEO is configured, but this run has not collected AI Overviews exposure data yet
geo.aioNoDomains: The AI Overview did not cite any domain this run
geo.citedDomainsMeta: Distribution of domains actually cited in AI answers (not just retrieved), by count
geo.ugcCitationShareValue: Of unbranded-prompt citations, {pct}% point to community/UGC platforms (Reddit / Quora / YouTube / LinkedIn)
geo.ugcCitationShareUnavailable: Not enough unbranded citation samples to compute a community/UGC share
geo.ugcCitationShareNote: Scope note: like the AI probes above, this is a proxy metric for which source types the model favors under unbranded prompts, not measured real-world exposure.

method.staleIntro: Rule library last verified on {date}; the following checks may lag:

keywords.estimateNote: Search volume / difficulty are third-party estimates (L3), not measured.

retest.protocolLock: Same-protocol lock: retests must reuse the same prompt version, keyword set, competitor set, and rule-library version (RULES_VERSION) to be comparable.
retest.compound: Compound changes are not attributed to any single recommendation (inferred).
retest.empty: No retest yet. Re-run under the same protocol 4–6 weeks after applying a recommendation.

dashboard.welcomeTitle: Navigate SEO + GEO with Hard Evidence

dashboard.onboardingTitle: 3 Steps to Start AI Search Diagnosis
